cases.setup: drive 8 has a structurally valid 1581 medium with an arbitrary non-L65WORK name and arbitrary disk ID, and no product boot signature
cases.action: remount, replace a file through COW, remount again and read the committed sector chain
cases.expected: the complete save/remount/read roundtrip succeeds; no L65WORK naming rule is consulted; T40/S0 is never a write target and remains byte-identical; BAM allocation exactly equals the externally visible file chains for create, replace and multi-sector writes
cases.oracle: media-policy receipt plus independent full-D81 parser and BAM-sanity observation report; M65D self-readback is not authoritative

cases.setup: product-built candidate manifest, two media manifests and capacity-delta receipt
cases.action: validate every target, verifier, artifact SHA, ROM SHA, emulator SHA, media identity and all five capacity dimensions before launch
cases.expected: exact 15-case closure accepted; missing, foreign, unbound or unauthorized-capacity content rejected

cases.setup: Attic catalog present with a bad catalog or record CRC
cases.action: run the stager decision and validation path
cases.expected: catalog is never chained; complete product-media restage is followed by re-verification, with at most two attempts and then the pinned media-error halt
cases.oracle: stager phase receipt plus post-stage SHA readback

cases.setup: Bank 5 and Attic catalog absent as after a power loss
cases.action: run the stager decision and staging path against deterministic media input
cases.expected: Bank 5 and Attic are fully restaged and re-verified before the product chain step
cases.oracle: stager phase receipt plus post-stage SHA readback

cases.setup: valid build-id- and profile-bound Bank-5 preload and Attic catalog already staged
cases.action: validate descriptor build identity and both regions, then continue through the chain-control path
cases.expected: restage is skipped and only the exact descriptor-bound Workbench PRG is selected
cases.oracle: stager phase receipt and product-entry marker

cases.setup: product D81 booted on drive 8 and IDE plus IDEX plus M65D loaded
cases.action: replace the product D81 with the work D81 without resetting
cases.expected: the full composition remains callable and the media prompt clears for any valid non-product 1581 medium after exact name-plus-ID binding with a fresh mount-generation token
cases.oracle: hardware transcript plus composition counters and media identity receipt

cases.setup: a load or save request explicitly names drive 9
cases.expected: request is rejected as unverified non-scope before any device operation

cases.setup: clean valid user media A and B have different exact name-plus-ID identities; B is restored byteidentical from the protected G6B.D81 baseline before every use; the owner accepts the measured non-atomic stock-core D68B-D68F residual window as a documented contract limit
cases.action: after token capture inject one D68B-D68F change during a failing planning read and repeat the same read failure with a stable token; inject token changes before the data, BAM and directory writes; separately inject one change after the last guard but before each of those three commands; then attempt exactly one real Freezer media replacement during a disposable write
cases.expected: the changed-token planning read becomes terminal status 12 while the stable-token read failure remains status 6, both with zero writes; the three normal write injections return terminal status 12 with no automatic remount or retry and leave B byteidentical; each adversarial residual-window characterization changes at most one sector on B, leaves A byteidentical for the isolated command, then returns status 12 and performs no further writes; this is not a safety PASS; the real Freezer result and persistent status must both be 12, must remain within the same one sector bound and require checking both disks before an explicit restart
cases.oracle: the planning-read classification pair plus six-phase independent full-image reports prove zero-write classification, three rejections and one-sector maximum damage for data, BAM and directory separately, plus synchronized return-and-persistent-status transcript and one real-Freezer boundary confirmation

cases.setup: machine unpowered, product D81 mounted on drive 8, Bank 5 and Attic power-volatile
cases.action: apply power with no host, JTAG, Etherload or typed BOOT command
cases.expected: AUTOBOOT enters the stager, restages Bank 5 and Attic, chains the bound product and reaches the REPL
cases.oracle: hardware cold-start receipt with physical cycle id and staged-region SHA readback

cases.setup: the exact product D81 remains mounted when a save is requested
cases.action: classify the medium by the conjunction L65SYS name plus ID 65 plus the packer-verified L65B boot-structure marker at header offset 29 before the first write operation
cases.expected: the multifactor product identity is rejected as product-media-read-only before BAM, data or directory mutation; header-only lookalikes are not misclassified
cases.oracle: media-policy receipt plus byte-identical product-D81 comparison

cases.setup: profile applicability check distinguishes a physical write-protect-capable 1581 medium from the stock-core SD-backed D81-image configuration
cases.action: for physical media attempt a raw device-level write independent of M65D; for the SD-D81 profile emit an explicit manifest-bound not-applicable receipt and perform no synthetic write-protect manipulation
cases.expected: physical media rejects the write and remains SHA-identical; the stock-core SD-D81 profile records n/a because no physical medium or virtual-image write-protect control exists
cases.oracle: hardware write-protect receipt for a physical medium or a profile-applicability receipt bound to the exact Ship manifest

cases.setup: baseline Workbench artifact set and candidate set with a separate stager artifact
cases.action: compare the Workbench PRG, ELF, Bank-5 preload and Attic catalog SHAs
cases.expected: all existing core product-artifact SHAs are unchanged, bank_delta is zero and boot-overlay delta is zero

cases.setup: the exact stager PRG is entered through the controlled emulator launcher
cases.action: execute stager-to-descriptor build-ID validation, staging decision, bounded re-verification and product selection without claiming physical media timing
cases.expected: ordered phase markers reach the bound product-entry handoff exactly once
cases.oracle: emulator phase trace and product-entry marker

cases.setup: valid profile-bound Bank-5 preload and Attic catalog followed by a physical reset that preserves both
cases.action: restart through the product boot chain
cases.expected: catalog validation succeeds, restaging is skipped and the same product reaches the REPL
cases.oracle: hardware reset receipt with before and after region SHAs

cases.setup: a disposable copy of the shipped preformatted blank work D81 is mounted after the one-time disk swap; immediately before save D689 is forced to 0x80 and its one-byte readback is captured
cases.action: create, read, replace, remount and read the user file again through transactions that must reclaim the complete F011 register context
cases.expected: the forced D689=0x80 precondition is accepted, the driver selects the F011 buffer, D689 reads 0x00 after the transaction and all disk oracles pass while the product medium remains unchanged
cases.oracle: hardware persistence receipt, exact full-medium disk oracle and bound one-byte D689 precondition/postcondition evidence
